title: ulex minor var. lusitanicus
intro: Ulex minor var. lusitanicus, also known as the dwarf gorse or Portuguese furze, is a variety of the Ulex minor shrub, native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve digestive issues such as bloating and constipation. The health benefits of Ulex minor var. lusitanicus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making it a valuable remedy for conditions such as arthritis and varicose veins. In herbal preparations, Ulex minor var. lusitanicus can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
